title: Songs of Conquest Mobile Beginner Guide: First Campaign Decisions That Matter
guidePromise: Helps new mobile players understand the first campaign as a sequence of map, economy, Wielder, and combat decisions instead of rushing every objective.
allowedClaims: Songs of Conquest Mobile is a mobile strategy game with Wielders, armies, resources, towns, and tactical battles.
A beginner guide can focus on reading objectives, preserving troops, using Wielders deliberately, and keeping the economy understandable.
Mobile play benefits from clear session goals because strategy maps can involve exploration, town checks, and tactical fights.
blockedClaims: PC campaign assumptions as mobile fact

playerPainPoints.guideQuestion: What should a new player focus on during the first mobile campaign session?
playerPainPoints.safeUse: Turn into a first-screen decision guide about objectives, Wielder safety, economy checks, and fight selection.
playerPainPoints.blockedUse: Do not claim a perfect opening route or exact campaign sequence.

notes: Recommended as SOCM-5 article 1. It can be broad and useful without ranking or formula claims.

title: Songs of Conquest Mobile Faction Choice Guide: How To Pick A Learning Path
guidePromise: Helps players choose a faction by learning curve, army identity, resource pressure, and tactical comfort rather than by rankings.
allowedClaims: The game has factions and armies that can support different play patterns.
A safe faction article can teach comparison criteria without publishing a power ranking.
Mobile players may benefit from choosing a faction whose army and Wielder decisions are easy to read in shorter sessions.
blockedClaims: DLC faction availability without mobile verification

playerPainPoints.guideQuestion: How should a new player choose a faction if rankings are not reliable yet?
playerPainPoints.safeUse: Use as a framework for comparing learning curve, unit readability, resource pressure, and tactical style.
playerPainPoints.blockedUse: Do not publish faction order or competitive verdicts.

notes: Recommended as SOCM-5 article 2, but must avoid ranking language in public body.

title: Songs of Conquest Mobile Combat Guide: Positioning, Threats, and Army Preservation
guidePromise: Helps mobile players treat tactical battles as army-preservation puzzles instead of trading units carelessly.
allowedClaims: Songs of Conquest includes tactical battles with armies and Wielders.
Beginner combat advice can focus on preserving important troops, reading threats, avoiding unnecessary trades, and entering fights with a clear purpose.
Mobile combat guides should prioritize readable decisions over exact damage math until mobile-specific evidence is stronger.

playerPainPoints.guideQuestion: How do you avoid losing too many troops in early battles?
playerPainPoints.safeUse: Turn into a guide about fight selection, protecting key stacks, checking threat range, and leaving fights that do not advance the objective.
playerPainPoints.blockedUse: Do not provide exact matchup formulas or solved army compositions.

notes: Recommended as SOCM-5 article 3. Good search potential and easy to keep mobile-safe.

title: Songs of Conquest Mobile Resource Guide: Town Building Without Losing Map Tempo
guidePromise: Helps players connect town upgrades, resource pressure, and map movement without publishing a fixed build order.
allowedClaims: The game includes resource and town or empire-building pressure.
A beginner economy guide can teach players to check what the town needs, what the map can provide, and whether the Wielder can keep exploring safely.
Resource planning should be framed as tradeoffs, not exact formulas.
blockedClaims: PC-only economy assumptions as mobile fact

playerPainPoints.guideQuestion: How do you build a town without falling behind on the map?
playerPainPoints.safeUse: Use as a decision guide about balancing exploration, resource pickups, and town spending.
playerPainPoints.blockedUse: Do not claim exact opening build orders.

notes: Good SOCM-5 or SOCM-6 article once the first three are live.

title: Songs of Conquest Mobile Wielder Guide: Roles, Magic, and Army Support
guidePromise: Helps players understand Wielders as army and magic support pieces, not just map units.
allowedClaims: Wielders are a core named system in Songs of Conquest.
A beginner guide can explain role identity, army support, spell timing, and why Wielder development should match the army plan.
Exact builds and strongest Wielder claims need stronger mobile-specific evidence.

playerPainPoints.guideQuestion: How should a beginner think about Wielder development?
playerPainPoints.safeUse: Use as a role and decision framework tied to army support and map goals.
playerPainPoints.blockedUse: Do not publish build rankings or exact skill trees.

notes: Useful systems article, but needs careful writing to avoid build-ranking drift.

title: Songs of Conquest Mobile Session Guide: Plan Turns, Towns, and Battles On A Phone
guidePromise: Helps mobile players split strategy play into readable sessions: map checks, town decisions, army movement, and tactical battles.
allowedClaims: The mobile version is available through mobile store pages.
A mobile session guide can focus on how to organize play decisions without assuming a specific control scheme beyond mobile play context.
Short-session planning is a player-facing usability guide, not a balance claim.
blockedClaims: specific control shortcuts not verified on mobile

playerPainPoints.guideQuestion: How do you keep a strategy map readable during shorter mobile play sessions?
playerPainPoints.safeUse: Use as a mobile-first planning guide for ending sessions at stable decision points.
playerPainPoints.blockedUse: Do not copy review complaints or claim universal mobile control problems.

notes: Useful differentiator for a mobile-focused site because it is not just a PC guide rewritten for phones.
